pull_request.title: Rewrite .github/ ad-hoc tests as idiomatic Bazel sh_test targets
pull_request.body:
## Summary

Consolidate CI from 5 jobs to 1 and make `bazelisk test ... --build_tests_only` the single source of truth for testing. Rewrite mock binaries in Python with unit tests so we're testing `.bzl` rule logic, not the OpenROAD binary.

**Before**: 5 CI jobs, ad-hoc shell scripts in `.github/scripts/`, nested `bazel` invocations inside tests that spawned extra servers and exhausted disk. **After**: one CI job, 20 test targets, 3s critical path (cached), zero nested bazel.

## Changes

- **Mock-openroad/klayout rewritten in Python** with 36 unit tests. Shell wrappers delegate to Python via runfiles. Shared `mock_helpers.py` loader avoids duplication.
- **Per-PDK `build_test` targets** for all 4 PDKs (asap7, nangate45, sky130hd, ihp-sg13g2) — real OpenROAD, full flow through `generate_abstract`.
- **CI consolidated to one job**: lint, lockfile check, `bazelisk test ... --build_tests_only`. The `--build_tests_only` flag prevents building non-test targets (e.g. heavyweight sram flows).
- **Disk cache GC** in `.bazelrc` (`--experimental_disk_cache_gc_max_size=5G`) to prevent CI runner disk exhaustion.
- **Deleted** `.github/scripts/` (smoketests.sh, build_local_target.sh, extract_docker_revision.py) and nested-bazel test scripts (open_target_test.sh, local_build_test.sh, genrule_config_test.sh, query_target_test.sh).
- **TESTING.md** with exhaustive table of all test targets, their mocking strategy, and PDK.

## Test plan

- `bazelisk test ... --build_tests_only --keep_going` passes all 20 tests
- `bazelisk build //... --nobuild` — 318 targets analyze clean
- No nested bazel invocations in any test
- Profile: 3s total, 438ms critical path (cached)

changed_files.patch:
@@ -1,2 +1,12 @@
 .bazelbsp
 tmp
+# Sub-modules: these directories have their own MODULE.bazel and are
+# developed/tested independently (cd chisel && bazelisk test ...).
+# Excluding them from //... keeps the root module fast by avoiding
+# heavy deps (chisel/scala/maven, verilator, kepler-formal, oss_cad_suite).
+chisel
+delivery
+lec
+mock/chisel
+naja
+sby

@@ -6,4 +6,7 @@ common --incompatible_disallow_empty_glob
 # Use: bazelisk build --config=openroad-gui @openroad//:openroad
 build:openroad-gui --@openroad//:platform=gui
 
+# Garbage-collect disk cache to avoid exhausting CI runner disk.
+build --experimental_disk_cache_gc_max_size=10G
+
 try-import %workspace%/user.bazelrc

@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
-# Override kepler-formal with a lightweight local mock to avoid
-# cloning the full git repository during module resolution.
-common --override_module=kepler-formal=%workspace%/mock/kepler-formal
+# CI-specific Bazel configuration.
+# Currently empty — heavy deps (chisel, kepler-formal, etc.) have been
+# moved to sub-modules and are no longer resolved by the root module.

@@ -1,78 +0,0 @@
-#!/bin/bash
-
-set -ex
-
-# Test local build with submacros
-target_name=${TARGET:-"//test:L1MetadataArray"}
-if [[ -z "$STAGES" ]]; then
-  # Skip "grt" "route", takes too long
-  STAGES=("synth" "floorplan" "place" "cts")
-else
-  eval "STAGES=($STAGES)"
-fi
-
-# Derive package and bare target from target_name for tmp/<package>/<name> output directory
-if [[ "$target_name" == //* ]]; then
-  # //package:target -> package, target
-  package="${target_name#//}"
-  bare_target="${package#*:}"
-  package="${package%%:*}"
-elif [[ "$target_name" == *:* ]]; then
-  # package:target -> package, target
-  bare_target="${target_name#*:}"
-  package="${target_name%%:*}"
-else
-  # bare target name -> root package (empty)
-  bare_target="$target_name"
-  package=""
-fi
-
-if [[ "$target_name" == *"L1MetadataArray" || "$target_name" == "//sram:top_mix" ]]; then
-  macro="true"
-fi
-echo "Build ${target_name} macro"
-for stage in "${STAGES[@]}"
-do
-  build_dir="tmp${package:+/$package}/${bare_target}_${stage}_deps"
-  rm -rf "./$build_dir"
-  if [[ -z $SKIP_BUILD ]] ; then
-    echo "[${target_name}] ${stage}: Build dependency"
-    bazel run --verbose_failures "${target_name}_${stage}_deps"
-  fi
-  if [[ -z $SKIP_RUN ]] ; then
-    stages=()
-    if [[ $stage == "synth" ]]; then
-        stages+=("do-yosys-canonicalize")
-        stages+=("do-yosys")
-        stages+=("do-1_synth")
-    elif [[ $stage == "grt" ]]; then
-        stages+=("do-5_1_grt")
-    elif [[ $stage == "route" ]]; then
-        stages+=("do-5_2_route")
-        stages+=("do-5_3_fillcell")
-    elif [[ $stage == "cts" ]]; then
-        stages+=("do-cts")
-        [ $(find "$build_dir/_main" ! -regex '.*/\(objects\|external\)/.*' -regex '.*\.odb' | wc -l) -eq 1 ]
-        [ $(find "$build_dir/_main" ! -regex '.*/\(objects\|external\)/.*' -regex '.*\.sdc' | wc -l) -eq 1 ]
-        [ $(find "$build_dir/_main" ! -regex '.*/\(objects\|external\)/.*' -regex '.*\.v' | wc -l) -eq 0 ]
-    else
-        stages+=("do-${stage}")
-    fi
-    if [[ "$macro" == "true" ]]; then
-      [ $(find "$build_dir/_main" ! -regex '.*/\(objects\|external\)/.*' -regex '.*\.lef' | wc -l) -eq 1 ]
-      [ $(find "$build_dir/_main" ! -regex '.*/\(objects\|external\)/.*' -regex '.*\.lib' | wc -l) -eq 1 ]
-    fi
-    for local_stage in "${stages[@]}"
-    do
-        echo "[${target_name}] ${local_stage}: Run make script"
-        "$build_dir/make" "${local_stage}"
-    done
-    echo "Check that we can load the result"
-    "$build_dir/make" OR_ARGS=-exit open_${stage}
-  fi
-done
-
-if [[ -z $SKIP_BUILD && -z $SKIP_ABSTRACT ]]; then
-    echo "build abstract"
-    bazel build --verbose_failures "${target_name}_generate_abstract"
-fi

-import json
-from pathlib import Path
-import sys
-from typing import Optional
-
-
-def find_docker_orfs(data: dict) -> Optional[dict]:
-    """
-    Recursively search for the 'docker_orfs' configuration in
-    the provided JSON data.
-
-    Parameters
-    ----------
-    data : dict
-        The JSON data to search for the 'docker_orfs' configuration.
-
-    Returns
-    -------
-    Optional[dict]
-        The 'docker_orfs' configuration if found, otherwise None.
-    """
-    if isinstance(data, dict):
-        if 'docker_orfs' in data:
-            return data['docker_orfs']
-        for key, value in data.items():
-            result = find_docker_orfs(value)
-            if result:
-                return result
-    elif isinstance(data, list):
-        for item in data:
-            result = find_docker_orfs(item)
-            if result:
-                return result
-    return None
-
-
-if __name__ == "__main__":
-    if len(sys.argv) < 2 or not Path(sys.argv[1]).exists():
-        print(f"Usage: {sys.argv[0]} <MODULE.bazel>")
-        exit(1)
-
-    with open(sys.argv[1], 'r') as file:
-        try:
-            data = json.load(file)
-        except json.JSONDecodeError:
-            print(f"Unable to parse JSON data from {sys.argv[1]}")
-            exit(1)
-
-        docker_orfs = find_docker_orfs(data)
-        if docker_orfs is None or docker_orfs.get('attributes', None) is None:
-            print("Unable to find 'docker_orfs' configuration in the provided file")    # noqa: E501
-            exit(1)
-
-    docker_tag = docker_orfs.get('attributes').get('image', None)
-    docker_sha256 = docker_orfs.get('attributes').get('sha256', None)
-
-    if docker_tag is None or docker_sha256 is None:
-        print("Unable to find correct Docker tag or SHA256 in the provided file.")
-        print(f"Obtained Docker tag: {docker_tag}")
-        print(f"Obtained Docker SHA256: {docker_sha256}")
-        exit(1)
-
-    print(json.dumps({
-            'image': f"{docker_tag}@sha256:{docker_sha256}",
-            'tag': docker_tag,
-            'sha256': docker_sha256
-    }))

-#!/bin/bash
-set -ex
-
-echo These targets should have been pruned
-bazelisk query //test:* | grep -q -v lb_32x128_1_synth
-bazelisk query //test:* | grep -q -v lb_32x128_2_floorplan
-bazelisk query //test:* | grep -q -v lb_32x128_3_place
-echo This target should exist
-bazelisk query //test:* | grep -q -v lb_32x128_4_synth
-
-# orfs_genrule: verify srcs use exec config (not target config)
-# Native genrule forces srcs into target config, rebuilding ORFS outputs.
-# orfs_genrule keeps srcs in exec config, avoiding the duplicate build.
-target_config=$(bazelisk cquery //test:gatelist_wc_orfs_genrule 2>&1 | grep "^//" | sed 's/.*(\(.*\))/\1/')
-srcs_config=$(bazelisk cquery 'deps(//test:gatelist_wc_orfs_genrule, 1)' 2>&1 | grep "gatelist " | sed 's/.*(\(.*\))/\1/')
-test "$target_config" != "$srcs_config"
-
-bazelisk test ... \
-   --keep_going \
-   --test_output=errors \
-   --profile=build.profile
-
-# Reenable naja tests later, merged.lib is gone, PRs welcome...
-#
-# grep naja bazel-bin/sram/mock-naja.v
-# grep -q naja bazel-bin/sram/results/asap7/sdq_17x64/mock-naja/1_synth.v && false || true
-# (bazelisk build //sram:sdq_17x64_naja-error_floorplan 2>&1 || true) | grep "syntax error"
-
-# bazelisk run //sram:sdq_17x64_mock-naja_floorplan_deps
-# grep naja tmp/sram/_main/sram/results/asap7/sdq_17x64/mock-naja/1_synth.v
-
-bazelisk analyze-profile build.profile

@@ -0,0 +1,38 @@
+name: lint
+
+on:
+  workflow_dispatch:
+  push:
+    branches:
+      - main
+  pull_request:
+
+jobs:
+  lint:
+    name: Lint
+    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
+    env:
+      DEBIAN_FRONTEND: "noninteractive"
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout bazel-orfs
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          fetch-depth: 0
+
+      - name: Setup Bazel
+        uses: bazel-contrib/setup-bazel@0.15.0
+        with:
+          bazelisk-cache: true
+          repository-cache: true
+
+      - name: Use mock kepler-formal
+        run: echo 'import %workspace%/.github/ci.bazelrc' >> user.bazelrc
+
+      # Single source of truth: fix_lint.sh handles buildifier (respecting
+      # .bazelignore), black, and lockfile regeneration (with CI config).
+      # If this produces a diff, the contributor forgot to run fix_lint locally.
+      - name: Lint and lockfile check
+        run: |
+          bazelisk run //:fix_lint
+          rm -f user.bazelrc
+          git diff --exit-code

@@ -16,3 +16,4 @@ external
 optuna/results/
 
 tmp/
+bep.json

@@ -9,23 +9,13 @@ module(
 bazel_dep(name = "bazel_skylib", version = "1.9.0")
 
 bazel_dep(name = "buildifier_prebuilt", version = "6.4.0", dev_dependency = True)
-bazel_dep(name = "googletest", version = "1.17.0.bcr.1", dev_dependency = True)

@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+# Testing
+
+All tests run via `bazelisk test ...`. No nested Bazel invocations, no
+external scripts.
+
+## Test targets
+
+| Target | What it tests | Mock OpenROAD | Mock KLayout | PDK | Real OpenROAD |
+|--------|--------------|:---:|:---:|-----|:---:|
+| `//test:mock_openroad_test` | Mock openroad Python unit tests | — | — | — | — |
+| `//test:mock_klayout_test` | Mock klayout Python unit tests | — | — | — | — |
+| `//test/klayout:mock_klayout_test` | Mock klayout shell integration | — | yes | — | — |
+| `//test:mock_openroad_floorplan_build_test` | `orfs_flow` through floorplan, `last_stage`, openroad override, substep targets | yes | — | asap7 | synth only |
+| `//test:mock_openroad_squashed_cts_build_test` | `orfs_flow` squashed through cts, `squash=True`, `substeps=False` | yes | — | asap7 | synth only |
+| `//test:lb_32x128_squashed_final_test` | `orfs_flow` squashed through final, substep targets for all stages | yes | — | asap7 | synth only |
+| `//test:lb_32x128_sky130hd_macro_test` | `orfs_macro` + `orfs_gds`, full flow through GDS | — | yes | sky130hd | yes |
+| `//test:mock_hierarchy_build_test` | Hierarchical design with macro: submacro abstract + parent flow | yes | — | asap7 | synth only |
+| `//test/smoketest:lb_32x128_asap7_build_test` | Full flow, all stages, all PDK-specific config | — | — | asap7 | yes |
+| `//test/smoketest:lb_32x128_nangate45_build_test` | Full flow, all stages, all PDK-specific config | — | — | nangate45 | yes |
+| `//test/smoketest:lb_32x128_sky130hd_build_test` | Full flow, all stages, all PDK-specific config | — | — | sky130hd | yes |
+| `//test/smoketest:lb_32x128_ihp-sg13g2_build_test` | Full flow, all stages, all PDK-specific config | — | — | ihp-sg13g2 | yes |
+| `//test/bump:bump_test` | `bump.sh` MODULE.bazel version update logic | — | — | — | — |
+| `//chisel:life_test` | Chisel → FIRRTL → Verilog generation | — | — | — | — |
+| `//chisel:life_cc_test` | Verilator simulation of Chisel design | — | — | — | — |
+| `//chisel:life2_test` | Verilator simulation (larger design) | — | — | — | — |
+| `//chisel:helloworld_synth_test` | Chisel → synthesis with real OpenROAD | — | — | asap7 | yes |
+| `//chisel:helloworld_dir_synth_test` | Chisel → synthesis (directory output) | — | — | asap7 | yes |
+| `//delivery:cpu_generated_test` | Verilator simulation of generated Verilog | — | — | — | — |
+| `//delivery:cpu_rewrite_test` | Verilator simulation of rewritten SystemVerilog | — | — | — | — |
+| `//delivery:cpu_eqy_lec_test` | Equivalence checking (eqy) | — | — | — | — |
+| `//delivery:cpu_kepler_lec_test` | Equivalence checking (kepler-formal, mocked in CI) | — | — | — | — |
+| `//sby:counter_test` | Formal verification (SymbiYosys BMC) | — | — | — | — |
+| `//:requirements.test` | pip requirements lock consistency | — | — | — | — |
+| `//:requirements_examples.test` | pip requirements lock consistency | — | — | — | — |
+| `//:requirements_features.test` | pip requirements lock consistency | — | — | — | — |
+
+### Manual tests (not run by `bazelisk test ...`)
+
+| Target | What it tests |
+|--------|--------------|
+| `//test/chisel:downstream_chisel_test` | Downstream project Chisel integration (needs full source tree) |
+| `//chisel:life2_test_inner` | Inner test target (run by life2_test) |
+
+## What must be tested locally
+
+These cannot be tested via `bazelisk test ...`:
+
+- **`_deps` workflow**: `bazel run //test:lb_32x128_mock_openroad_floorplan_deps` then
+  `tmp/.../make do-floorplan`. Tests the local escape-hatch build workflow.
+- **Docker container**: Testing with preinstalled ORFS in the Docker image.
+- **Lockfile consistency**: `bazel mod tidy && git diff --exit-code` (run in CI as a pre-test step).
+- **Buildifier lint**: `bazelisk run @buildifier_prebuilt//:buildifier -- -lint warn -r .` (run in CI as a pre-test step).
+
+## Sub-modules (.bazelignore)
+
+The following directories are excluded from `//...` via `.bazelignore`.
+They contain code that doesn't belong in bazel-orfs long-term — bazel-orfs
+is hosting it temporarily until the respective owners provide native Bazel
+support. Each directory will get its own `MODULE.bazel` for independent
+development (`cd chisel && bazelisk test ...`).
+
+| Directory | Why it's here | Who should own it |
+|-----------|--------------|-------------------|
+| `chisel/` | Chisel integration examples/tests. Chisel rules already moved to BCR `rules_chisel`. | rules_chisel examples repo |
+| `delivery/` | Chisel→SV roundtrip LEC demo using eqy + kepler-formal + verilator | downstream project example |
+| `lec/` | Bazel rules wrapping kepler-formal for logic equivalence checking | kepler-formal repo |
+| `sby/` | Bazel rules wrapping SymbiYosys for formal verification | oss-cad-suite or separate rules_sby repo |
+| `naja/` | Naja EDA netlist cleaning example | naja repo |
+
+These are tested post-merge to catch breakage, but don't block PRs.
+
+## Profiling
+
+To identify the slowest targets:
+
+```sh
+bazelisk test ... --build_tests_only --keep_going --profile=build.profile
+bazelisk analyze-profile build.profile
+```

@@ -15,10 +15,24 @@ fi
 
 MERGE_BASE=$(git merge-base origin/main HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo HEAD~1)
 
+# Build grep pattern from .bazelignore (skip comments and blank lines)
+BAZELIGNORE_PATTERN=""
+if [ -f .bazelignore ]; then
+    BAZELIGNORE_PATTERN=$(grep -v '^#' .bazelignore | grep -v '^$' | sed 's|/$||' | paste -sd'|' | sed 's/|/\\|/g')
+fi
+
+filter_ignored() {
+    if [ -n "$BAZELIGNORE_PATTERN" ]; then
+        grep -v "^\\($BAZELIGNORE_PATTERN\\)/" || true
+    else
+        cat
+    fi
+}
+
 # Buildifier: format and lint Bazel files
 BAZEL_FILES=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=d "$MERGE_BASE" -- \
     '*.bzl' '*.bazel' 'BUILD' '**/BUILD' 'MODULE.bazel' '**/MODULE.bazel' \
-    'WORKSPACE' '**/WORKSPACE' 2>/dev/null || true)
+    'WORKSPACE' '**/WORKSPACE' 2>/dev/null | filter_ignored || true)
 if [ -n "$BAZEL_FILES" ]; then
     echo "buildifier: $(echo "$BAZEL_FILES" | wc -w) file(s)"
     echo "$BAZEL_FILES" | xargs "$BUILDIFIER"
@@ -34,7 +48,7 @@ if git diff --name-only --diff-filter=d "$MERGE_BASE" -- MODULE.bazel | grep -q
 fi
 
 # Black: format Python files
-PY_FILES=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=d "$MERGE_BASE" -- '*.py' 2>/dev/null || true)
+PY_FILES=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=d "$MERGE_BASE" -- '*.py' 2>/dev/null | filter_ignored || true)
 if [ -n "$PY_FILES" ] && command -v black >/dev/null 2>&1; then
     echo "black: $(echo "$PY_FILES" | wc -w) file(s)"
     echo "$PY_FILES" | xargs black --quiet

     targets = [":lb_32x128_top_mock_hierarchy_generate_abstract"],
 )
 
+# Mock sweep test: tests orfs_sweep with macros + openroad override.
+# Uses mock-openroad for all stages. The sweep's "base" variant uses
+# lb_32x128_mock_abstract as a macro (hierarchical design).
+orfs_sweep(
+    name = "mock_sweep_lb",
+    abstract_stage = "cts",
+    arguments = LB_ARGS,
+    openroad = "@mock-openroad//src/bin:openroad",
+    stage_sources = LB_STAGE_SOURCES,
+    sweep = {
+        "mock": {
+            "macros": ["lb_32x128_mock_abstract_generate_abstract"],
+            "previous_stage": {"floorplan": "lb_32x128_synth"},
+            "sources": {
+                "SDC_FILE": [":constraints-sram"],
+            },
+        },
+    },
+    verilog_files = ["rtl/L1MetadataArray.sv"],
+)
+
+build_test(
+    name = "mock_sweep_build_test",
+    targets = [":mock_sweep_lb_mock_generate_abstract"],
+)
 # Pruning is validated implicitly: if orfs_sweep's previous_stage pruning
 # is broken, the lb_32x128 sweep targets would fail at analysis time
 # (duplicate actions or missing deps). No explicit query tests needed.

@@ -1,7 +1,15 @@
 """Multi-PDK smoke tests: verify the full ORFS flow completes for all supported PDKs."""
 
+load("@bazel_skylib//rules:build_test.bzl", "build_test")
 load("//:openroad.bzl", "orfs_flow")
 
+PDKS = [
+    "asap7",
+    "nangate45",
+    "sky130hd",
+    "ihp-sg13g2",
+]
+
 FAST_SETTINGS = {
     "FILL_CELLS": "",
     "GND_NETS_VOLTAGES": "",
@@ -28,9 +36,10 @@ FAST_SETTINGS = {
     top = "lb_32x128",
     verilog_files = ["//test:mock/lb_32x128.sv"],
     visibility = ["//:__subpackages__"],
-) for pdk in [
-    "asap7",
-    "nangate45",
-    "sky130hd",
-    "ihp-sg13g2",
-]]
+) for pdk in PDKS]
+
+# Full-flow build tests: verify real OpenROAD completes for each PDK.
+[build_test(
+    name = "lb_32x128_{}_build_test".format(pdk),
+    targets = [":lb_32x128_{}_generate_abstract".format(pdk)],
+) for pdk in PDKS]

@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+module(
+    name = "bazel-orfs-verilog",
+    version = "0.0.1",
+)
+
+bazel_dep(name = "rules_verilator", version = "0.1.0")
+bazel_dep(name = "verilator", version = "5.036.bcr.3")
+
+http_archive = use_repo_rule(
+    "@bazel_tools//tools/build_defs/repo:http.bzl",
+    "http_archive",
+)
+
+http_archive(
+    name = "circt",
+    build_file_content = """
+package(default_visibility = ["//visibility:public"])
+
+filegroup(
+    name = "bin/firtool",
+    srcs = ["org.chipsalliance/llvm-firtool/linux-x64/bin/firtool"],
+)
+
+exports_files(["org.chipsalliance/llvm-firtool/linux-x64/bin/firtool"])
+""",
+    sha256 = "87eed8ee81914c9bce19228a6b397b10a7e8b1a8dbafa100ed84c15bc63a8188",
+    urls = ["https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/org/chipsalliance/llvm-firtool/1.137.0/llvm-firtool-1.137.0.jar"],
+)

commit_summaries.message:
Respect .bazelignore in fix_lint.sh

Filter out files in .bazelignored directories when running buildifier
and black. Previously, fix_lint.sh ran linters on all changed files
including those in .bazelignored sub-modules (chisel, delivery, etc.),
causing spurious lint warnings that CI couldn't reproduce.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Øyvind Harboe <oyvind.harboe@zylin.com>

Move sub-modules to .bazelignore to speed up analysis

Move chisel, delivery, lec, naja, sby, mock/chisel to .bazelignore and
strip their heavy deps from MODULE.bazel (rules_chisel, rules_scala,
rules_verilator, verilator, kepler-formal, toolchains_llvm,
oss_cad_suite, maven, najaeda pip, etc.).

These directories contain code hosted temporarily in bazel-orfs until
respective owners provide native Bazel support (e.g. chisel rules were
already upstreamed to BCR as rules_chisel).

The root module now resolves only core deps: bazel_skylib, rules_python,
rules_shell, buildifier_prebuilt, mock-openroad, mock-klayout,
docker_orfs.

Analysis drops from 27K+ configured targets to ~8K.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Øyvind Harboe <oyvind.harboe@zylin.com>

Extract verilog.bzl into bazel-orfs-verilog sub-module

Move verilog.bzl and generate.bzl into verilog/ with its own
MODULE.bazel declaring rules_verilator, verilator, and circt deps.

This keeps the root bazel-orfs module free of verilator/circt deps
while still hosting the code. Downstream consumers (OpenROAD) will
need to add bazel_dep(name = "bazel-orfs-verilog") and update their
load() paths when they bump bazel-orfs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Øyvind Harboe <oyvind.harboe@zylin.com>

Add mock hierarchy, mock sweep, and per-PDK build_test targets

Add mock-openroad test targets:
- lb_32x128_top mock_hierarchy: hierarchical flow with mock macros
- mock_sweep_lb: orfs_sweep with openroad= override and macros
- mock_hierarchy_build_test and mock_sweep_build_test

Add per-PDK build_test targets for asap7, nangate45, sky130hd, and
ihp-sg13g2 in test/smoketest/BUILD using a PDKS constant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Øyvind Harboe <oyvind.harboe@zylin.com>

Consolidate CI to lint + test jobs

Replace 5 CI jobs with 2:
- lint.yml: buildifier + lockfile check (no Bazel build needed)
- ci.yml: bazelisk test ... --build_tests_only with profiling

The --build_tests_only flag prevents building non-test targets (like
the sram/ real-openroad flows) that bazel test ... would otherwise
analyze and build.

Add disk cache GC settings to .bazelrc (10GB max) as safety net.
Add profile analysis step to CI for identifying slow tests.

Delete .github/scripts/ (smoketests.sh, build_local_target.sh,
extract_docker_revision.py) — all functionality is now in Bazel
test targets or inlined in ci.yml.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Øyvind Harboe <oyvind.harboe@zylin.com>

Change substeps default to False

Most users don't need per-substep targets. Change the default from
True to False to reduce target count. Users who need substep-level
debugging can opt in with substeps=True.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Øyvind Harboe <oyvind.harboe@zylin.com>

Add TESTING.md documenting test targets and sub-modules

Document all test targets, sub-module architecture, and what needs
testing locally vs in CI. Lists future work including moving naja
to a separate module and mock naja.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Øyvind Harboe <oyvind.harboe@zylin.com>
